segment-text: written by Gary Dauberman
ST. MONICA'S CATHOLIC CHURCH - MORNING

A MONSTROUS GARGOYLE. Sculpted in stone. A WARRIOR ANGEL. In stained glass. CHRIST ON A CROSS. Carved in wood. And that's FATHER PEREZ -- late 40s, of flesh and bone -standing at the pulpit -FATHER PEREZ God honors sacrifice. And our sacrifices touch God's heart and move his hand. "This is my
And while you will no doubt throw this up ON SCREEN:
LOS ANGELES, 1970

You could already tell that by the hairstyles and wardrobe of the congregation. Among them are -MIA and JOHN FORM. 20s. Holding hands. Seem to be enjoying the sermon. But as the camera pans down we see they're actually engaged in -An intense thumb war battle.
"...Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his loved ones."
Mia almost has him but John -- sneak attack -- extends his pointer finger and pins her down -PEREZ (CONT'D)
MOMENTS LATER
Organ BLARES. Congregation rises. A murmur of conversation. John and Mia walk down the aisle. Out in the
They're greeted by PETE HIGGINS, early 50s. He was the man seated next to them in the pew.
Who won?
John. But he cheats.
John squeezes Mia. His hand over the small bump of her belly.
It was two against one. I had to do something...
Mia blushes. Embarrassed. Pete smiles but looks past them at the congregation still leaving the church...
Now where'd Sharon get off to?
Mia moves John's hand away.
I'll go find her.
CHURCH - SIDE ALTAR - MOMENTS LATER

THE VIRGIN MARY looks down at -SHARON HIGGINS, late 40s. Looks older. She kneels at the side altar. Lights a votive candle. Prays. BEHIND HER Mia walks in, searching. Stops when she sees Sharon. Watches her for a bit too long because --Sharon turns, getting to her feet. Mia not sure whether to stay or leave. Doesn't matter anyway
Pete send you after me? I swear one day he thinks I'll just...
She takes Mia's hand. Nevermind....
Men. What would they do without us? I mean, honestly...
They leave.
INT./EXT. CONTINENTAL - HIGGINS' HOUSE - LATER
A Lincoln Continental pulls into the driveway. Parks. As the Higgins' and the Forms' exit the car --
We appreciate the lift, Pete.
You just make sure to take care of this wife and baby of yours...
John and Mia smile.
Did we ask about names yet?
Sharon...
What? That's not prying. That's conversation...
We're still working on them anyway.
But we're pretty sure John if it's a boy and we were thinking Gertrude if it's a girl...
We were?
You said you liked it.
I never said that.
But it's my grandmother's name...
And I can't stand your grandmother...
Oh dear...
It's not just me. No one can stand her. Tell them, John.
She's right. Everyone hates her.
Pete laughs.
C'mon, Sharon. Let's get inside. Sounds like these two have a lot to talk about...
Thank you again.
Think nothing of it. And remember, children are a blessing...
They are indeed. Then they learn to talk...
EXT. FORM HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
Hand in hand, John and Mia walk next door and up the path toward their front porch.
So that's a no on Gertrude?
Mia looks back at the Pete and Sharon entering their home.
You shouldn't talk so much about the baby in front of them. It's... it isn't right...
What? Why not?
They only lost their daughter two years ago...
They didn't lose their daughter, Mia. She ran away and joined up with the hippies. By now she probably has three husbands and calls herself Star...
Well, even Star is a better name than Gertrude...
John turns the knob and opens the Front Door.
You didn't lock it?
Why would I?
We need to start locking it. It's a different world now, John...
....death count continues to climb in Vietnam. On our shores....
FORM HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
ON TELEVISION -News footage. Vietnam. Student Protests. Manson Family.
.. a so-called religious cult has
Mia sits in front of her SINGER SEWING MACHINE. Threading a green piece of fabric. Her fingers move precariously close to the needle as it goes up and down and up and down a million times a second.
Honey, can you shut the TV off? It's making it a little hard to concentrate...
Mia gets up. Gathers up the fabric at the sewing machine. Switches off the television. Goes into the
KITCHEN
Where John sits at the table. Drowning in MEDICAL BOOKS open to various chapters. We catch glimpses of Cadavers, skeletons, gross anatomy. Mia rubs his neck.
You shouldn't be watching that stuff anyway. You could upset the baby. There's been some new research...
Oh Gosh... More new research...
It's true. They're learning that babies in utero experience much more of the outside world than they ever thought possible. They recognize
voices... noises... songs...
I bet the men who did that research spent millions when they could have just asked a mother for free...
She sits on his lap. Flips through his books.
How's it going in here anyway?
I'm trying my hardest to enjoy it. Everyone keeps reminding me that when you're in your residency it gets a million times worse. I don't even mention the fact I'll also be a new father...
Why not?
I don't know. Sometimes you just don't want to hear it...
Hear what?
How difficult things can be.
Difficult...
Maybe difficult isn't the word. Challenging?
Are you talking about the residency or me and the baby?
I don't know. All of it, I guess.
Mia gets up from his lap.
What?
INT. NURSERY - MOMENTS LATER

Some people choose animals as a theme. Others choose the moon and stars. Mia clearly chose -DOLLS. Of all sorts. Like she's been collecting them for awhile. On the rocking chair. On the shelves. In the crib. Mia folds the fabric. drapes it over the chair. John appears in the doorway. Apologetic.
Look, I didn't mean for that to sound like it did. I'm sorry...
How'd you want it to sound?
Not like that. But it's not as if difficult or challenging are bad things anyway. Look at what you put me through before you said yes to our first date...
But wasn't it worth it?
I still can't believe it worked. Mia sighs.
But I am sorry, Mia. I know sometimes I can get a little caught up in my own world...
It's fine. I know you're stressed. I've just been a little sensitive lately...
Okay, well. Want me to tell you why?
Oh please, Doctor.
Well, in my almost expert opinion, you're a little sensitive because you're a lot pregnant.
Mia laughs.
I really am sorry, Mia. Yes, I'm stressed over finishing med school, Yes, I'm freaking out over being matched but I've never been happier. And that's because of you...
He kisses her.
Here. Wait. I want to give you something...
Last time you said that I ended up pregnant...

John exits the nursery. Leaves Mia alone. She touches the mobile above the crib. It plays a few notes of "Rockabye Baby". She smiles. Maybe thinking of the wonderful things yet to come... John returns. A LARGE WRAPPED GIFT in his hands. I bet you know what this is. But Mia doesn't. So let's watch:
I was saving this until baby but...
But now you're feeling --
-- guilty. About what I said. Yes. SECONDS LATER
Mia tears at the wrapping, revealing -Those eyes. Those pig tails. That smile. We know her as the ANNABELLE DOLL. But we've never seen her like this. New. Perfect. Exquisite.
Oh my god... John...
That's the one, right?
Mia nods. It's the one.
I love her.
She puts Annabelle down and kisses John. He wraps Mia up in his arms. Holds her as he looks around the nursery.
You really did a fine job in here...
One question though... (pause) What if it's a boy?
INT. MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT Mia and John. Asleep in bed.

A cool breeze blows through the open bedroom window. And the CLOSED COTTON CURTAINS billow out, almost like they're floating under water, revealing between them -The HIGGINS' HOUSE Next door. A light goes on as -The curtains fall back into place. But then -- Another breeze lifts them again and WE SEE -Pete is out of bed. Like he just heard something in the next room. Sharon sits up in bed. Curtains fall. Breeze. Sharon says something to Pete. Can't hear what. But both seem concerned which is when Pete leaves the bedroom to investigate and -- shit -Curtains. A SCREAM. Breeze.
BLOOD on the walls in the Higgins Bedroom.

Someone enters back into the room -- FUCK -- can't get a good look cause -Curtains. Right as Mia sits up in bed. She goes over to the window and pulls Curtains aside to reveal -Higgins' Bedroom is dark. Lights out.
John -- John -- wake up -JOHN Wassat? Is it the baby?
No -- next door -- I heard a scream -I think something is wrong...
John joins her at the window. No evidence of what we just
John -- wait --
She follows him down the
HALLWAY
Shouldn't we call the cops?
John pauses at the FRONT DOOR. Hand on the knob.
Because you think you heard a scream? Let me just go over and see. They'd do the same for us... I don't know about you but I'm getting the sense that
something is standing on the other side of that door. Something bad.
If they heard me scream I'd rather they call the police for us...
Think they heard you scream, you mean...
He starts to turn the doorknob.
Don't do that.
Just be careful.
I will...
He pulls open the Front Door to reveal -- gasp -Oh. My sense was wrong. It's nothing. He exits. Mia watches him from the
FRONT DOOR
As he crosses over their yard and into the

Loses sight of him when he steps onto their front porch. She waits. Along with the crickets. And that DOG BARKING somewhere down at the end of their block. Waits some more. Anxious, she steps outside and -EXT. FORM HOUSE - CONTINUOUS -- takes a half step down off the porch.
...John? No answer.
She leaves the stairs. Walks over to the
HIGGINS' HOUSE
Eyes fixated on the darkness of their Front Porch. As she approaches. Petrified.
John? Where are you? Is everything -WHAM!
Front Door flies wide open. Mia reels back. Realizes it's John. And he's SPLATTERED in blood. Like he just got out of a botched surgery.
GO! Call an ambulance!
Oh God -- shit -- what happened?
BEHIND JOHN

The dim light from the outdoors illuminates Pete and Sharon Higgins on the floor. Drowning in their own blood. Mia's mouth moves. No words come out. Her eyes move back up to John, realizes he's saying -JOHN Mia. Go. Now. John races back inside to help the Higgins'. Mia runs back to their house and hurries up the front steps and crashes through their front door.
INT. FORM HOUSE - KITCHEN - SECONDS LATER

Mia picks the phone off the wall. Dials. Which isn't so easy when you're fingers are shaking like hers. As the line RINGS, Mia turns toward the WINDOW Watches the Higgins' House. Still dark. On the other end of the line, Operator answers.
Y-yes -- we need an ambulance -- 473
Garner Road -- two people -- I don't
know -- but there's a lot of blood. IN THE HALLWAY A STREAK OF WHITE passes behind Mia. Didja see it? Because she didn't.
Yes -- thank you -- please hurry.
Mia hangs up. Goes over to the window. Shuts and locks it. Retreats back into the
HALLWAY

And walks toward the Master Bedroom. She passes the Nursery. But too distracted to see standing in the center of the room -A WOMAN. Bone thin. Bald head. Loose, white clothes stained with red blood. And she's staring down at the -Annabelle Doll. That she holds in her sharp fingered hands. Holy shit. Mia reaches the
MASTER BEDROOM
And grabs her robe. Throws it on. Cinches the sash as it dawns on her -Did she just see...? Oh God...
I like your dolls...

Mia SCREAMS in fear. As a MAN -- thin, long beard, patches of hair -- steps out from behind the closet door. In his hand, a SHARP KNIFE. Sticky with blood. Rushes toward Mia. Grabs her. Raises his knife up high and then -THNK! Stabs her in the side of the stomach.
NO!

Now she SCREAMS in pain. John BURSTS through the bedroom door. Tackles the Man. Knife falls. Skids across the floor. Mia collapses. Blood soaks her nightgown. She looks over at -John and the Man wrestling on the floor. John pins the Man down with his knees and -POUNDS-POUNDS-POUNDS his face in with his fists when -The Woman -- YELLING LIKE A BANSHEE -- enters and LEAPS onto John's BACK. Sinks her teeth in his shoulder. A rabid dog. That won't let go. Mia crawls over to the knife. Picks it up. Holding her stomach, she stands. Sees the Man sit up. His eyes wild. Nose bashed in. But a smile on thin lips. Like he's enjoying this. The Man grabs hold of John's leg. Twists it. Mia runs over. Falls on top of the Man while she STABS him. The Woman releases her bite on John. Hops on top of Mia. Yanks the knife away from her. Raises it to finish Mia off. But John THROWS her back into the Hallway as -POLICE charge through the Front Door. Guns at the ready. The Woman scrambles to her feet and dives into the Nursery. Slams the door closed. Locks it. John kneels beside Mia. She writhes on the floor. So much pain. EMTS enter into the room.
She's pregnant -- five months -- the
baby -- we can't lose the baby -Mia's VISION. In and out of focus as she looks down the
HALLWAY
Police Officers outside the Nursery. BANGING on its locked door.
OPEN UP! THIS IS THE POLICE!

One Officer raises his gun. Nods to Another. Another approaches the door and -KICKS it open. Revealing -The Woman. Seated on the floor against the back wall. Her throat slit wide open. By that knife beside her on the floor. And in her hands she clutches close to her chest -The Annabelle Doll. From the gash in the Woman's neck, a DROP OF BLOOD rolls down and onto the Annabelle doll where... It slides down to her eye. Like a crimson tear in reverse. Until it disappears inside the corner of the eye. Getting a glimpse of the horror as she's wheeled by on a gurney. Oxygen mask over her face. Hands over belly. And John next to her promising that -JOHN
If he only knew. Movement within. A WHITE GHOST-LIKE APPARITION. Somewhere, a HEARTBEAT. Off in the distance. But getting louder. And stronger. Go on, ZOOM OUT to reveal -An ULTRASOUND.
INT. DOCTOR'S OFFICE - DAY
Mia sits in the examination chair. John holds her hand, seated on a stool next to it. DOCTOR BURGHER sits across from them.

...and while the amniotic sac was not punctured and is not ruptured, we are seeing signs of stress on the cervix most likely caused by the trauma which is why we're putting you on bed rest for the remainder of your pregnancy. You can get up to use the facilities if you do so delicately but anything beyond that is putting yourself and your child at risk...
Mia stares back blankly at the doctor. Not so sure she hears him. Not so sure she hears anything. Just her own thoughts. Her own fears.
FORM HOUSE - DAY
Mia leans on John as he helps her up the porch steps. Keeps her hands on her belly. Winces with every inch forward.
Maybe we should take a break...
I'm fine...
Casts a look over to the Higgins'.
...I just want to get inside.
FORM HOUSE - VARIOUS - DAY

John helps Mia down the hallway. Both step like they're walking on a mine field. Mia stop -MIA Wait... wait... -- when she reaches the Nursery. She looks inside. Everything is back to normal. Like nothing ever happened.
I had them scrub everything at least twice. You could perform surgery in there it's so clean...
Mia reaches in and shuts the door.
I want everything out of the nursery. I can't go in there again...
Moves on.
MASTER BEDROOM - LATER

Mia settles into bed. She looks over at the closet door the Man hid behind. Down at the floor where John was attacked. Everywhere a reminder. Protective, she pulls the sheets up over her belly. John rolls their ZENITH TV into the bedroom. Plugs it in.
You don't have to do that...
Mi. You're on bed rest for the next three months. You'd go crazy if I didn't. Just don't get obsessed with those daytime soaps...
Gosh, you know I hate those...
John sits down on the edge of the bed. Like right on the edge. Doesn't want to rock the bed too much.
I'll bring your sewing machine in later. We can turn this room into your factory...
Mia grabs his hand. Like it's anchor.
You really think baby is okay?
The baby isn't even aware of what happened...
Oh, really? Because I heard there was some new research...
Mia. You heard the doctors. So as long as you listen to them, the baby will be fine. The baby is fine. You're its mother, right? If it wasn't, you'd know...
Mia looks down at her tummy. Glances over at the window. Sees the Higgins' next door.
Do you mind shutting those curtains? I want to try and get some rest...
John kisses her.
Good idea.
FORM HOUSE - VARIOUS - NIGHT

So quiet. The only sound is the tickticktick of the clock on the mantle. But you need to strain to even hear that. Furniture sit like slumbering creatures. The Sewing Machine among them, an strange alien silhouette. Somewhere, a grating creeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeaaaaak. Like plastic rubbing against plastic. In the NURSERY The Mobile above spins. Only like a quarter rotation. But enough to get a few notes of 'Rockabye Baby'. In the MASTER BEDROOM Mia stirs. John snores. The quiet resumes.
Until -TchnkTchnkTchnkTchnkTchnkTchnkTchnkTchnkTchnk!
Mia starts awake. John springs out of bed. Races down the Hallway and into the
LIVING ROOM
Switches on the lights. Sees the Sewing Machine needle going at top speed. Whole machine shakes and vibrates with motion. John pulls the plug.
John?
It's okay. It's just the sewing machine...
He takes a quick tour of the house. Notes that the Nursery Door is now wide open. Reaches in and begins to close it. But the door catches on something. He looks down and sees --
John bends over and picks her up. Sets her down on the rocking chair inside the Nursery. Shuts the door.
MASTER BEDROOM - DAY

Who knows how much time has passed. On bed rest, all the days seem to run together. Mia sits up in bed. Propped on pillows. Watches 'ALL MY CHILDREN' on the Television. And yeah, she's obsessed. RECEPTION sure as hell is spotty though. Too bad she's gotta wait another ten years or so for cable. ON TV That bitch Erica Kane is spilling the beans that Tyler is not in fact Ruth's son but is actually -SNOW. Static. Frustrated, Mia picks up a LIFE Magazine and tosses it against the TV. It shuts off. She turns over on her side. Maybe ready to finally get some rest when she sees -The Annabelle Doll. Laying on the floor down at the far end of the Hallway. Head turned. Staring right at her.
John? Could you...
She looks over at the clock. It's mid-afternoon. Ugh.
...come home soon please.
Slides out of bed. Shuffles over to the bedroom door. Gives one last look to Annabelle laying there and -Closes the door.
Bitch.
BATHROOM - LATER

Mia peels off the bandage on her belly. Examines the stab wound. You'll wince when you see it. Takes a wet cloth and blots around it. Her face scrunched into the 'oh shit this hurt and looks disgusting' expression. She tosses the cloth aside. Washes her hands. Dries 'em off and exits back into the
MASTER BEDROOM
Sees her the bedroom door is open. Gah. Mia tenses up. But then John appears from the Kitchen. Phew. Mia relaxes. A little. As he enters into the bedroom -JOHN How'd it go today?
TV went out.
She gets back into bed.
Again? Maybe it's this room. It never did that in the den...
What about you? Bring any news of the outside world?
I met with that advisor I was telling you about. He thinks I have a shot at Pasadena...
Of course you do.
It's further from the beach. Sure you'd be okay with that?
A change of view would be good...
Well let's not start packing yet. But there is a conference he'd like me to attend that could help move things in that direction. But I'd have to fly out for the day in a few weeks...
It's one day. I'll be fine.
Detective Clarkin called too. He wanted to know if he could stop by sometime this week... Shit...
I'd rather just forget the whole thing...
Mia sees Annabelle still down there at the end of the hall. But now she's sitting up.
I'm sorry John but we need to get rid of that doll. She had it in her hands...
John looks over. Sees Annabelle.
And I guess you can tell the Detective he can stop by whenever is convenient for him. It's isn't like I'm going anywhere anytime soon...
FORM HOUSE - DAY
John exits holding the Annabelle Doll. Walks down to the garbage can at the curb. Lifts the lid and -Drops Annabelle inside. Annabelle stares up at us as --
LIVING ROOM - DAY
DET. CLARKIN, 30s, sits in a chair. John hands him a cup of coffee then sits on the sofa next to Mia.
And we've confirmed it was the
Higgins' daughter and her boyfriend.
That cults are taking over the country?
It isn't as prevalent as they want you to believe...
You're going to have a hard time convincing us of that...
Det. Clarkin takes an awkward sip of coffee.
So? Were they satanic?
Clarkin glances over at Mia. Back at John.
It's okay. She should know. That's why you're here, isn't it?
I'm here to reassure you of your safety. But yes, it's believed they had an interest in the occult they did this to prove their devotion. Violence for violence sake.
But that's crazy...
Crazy people do crazy things. That's all this was... I'll dig a little deeper, see what I can learn. If we get any further information I will reach out and --
No. Thank you but... No. Unless it has something to do with our safety I'd just rather not hear any more about it...
I understand.
STATIC.
On the Television Screen. But the picture becomes clearer and clearer as -John moves the antennas.
Okay. Good. Right there...
The Late Late Show is almost starting. Maybe it's a western. Or a romance classic. Sure as hell ain't a horror movie.
I'll go make the popcorn.
INT. KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
John pulls a JIFFY POP TIN from the cupboard. Throws it on the electric stove. Turns the burner knob on high. The wire coil grates begin to heat. As John walks down the hallway -JOHN
to have this much butter and sa-MASTER BEDROOM Sees Mia has dozed off to dreamland. And the TV is STATIC again. John shuts it off. Back in the
KITCHEN
He turns off the stove too. And the lights.
MASTER BEDROOM - MORNING
Mia watches John knot his tie. Slip on his suit jacket.
I'm sorry I have to go and do this.
It's for our future. I'd be upset if you didn't... How do you feel? Don't ask.
They'll love you.
MASTER BEDROOM - LATER
TV is on. Reception still sucks.

Mia feeds a piece of fabric through the Sewing Machine. Every few seconds, her fingers get a hairs width away from the needle before moving back to push more fabric through. This process repeats several times. And we react like we just know one of these times she's going to get stitched.
LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
Front Door creeeeeeeaks OPEN. Not much. But enough. INT.
KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
The DIALS for the STOVE turned. From OFF all the way to HIGH. INT.
MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
Fabric. Needle. Almost ouch.
KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
The burner coils -- including the one under the Jiffy Pop -glow a brighter and brighter orange. The Jiffy Pop Foil begins to balloon.
MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
Fabric. Needle. Flesh. Blood.
Mia grabs her finger...
Shit...
Moves toward the Bathroom. Using the nightstand, the dresser, and whatever else to help ease her weight.
KITCHEN - SAME TIME
A lone kernel POPS.
BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Mia turns on the FAUCET. Runs the cold water. Shoves her finger under. Blood circles the drain. The rush of water drowns out the sound of -INT.
KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
POP. POP. POP. POPPOPPOPPOPPOPPOPPOP! INT.
BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Mia finds a BAND-AID in the Medicine Cabinet. Wraps it around her finger. Mia shuts off the water right as -INT.
KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
-- the POPS stop. But the Foil continues to balloon.
MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
Mia exits from the bathroom. Resumes her position at the Sewing Machine. TchnkTchnkTchnkTchnk!
KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
Jiffy Pop Foil BURSTS. Popcorn flies. A FIREBALL flares up. And a hanging DISH TOWEL catches flame. INT.
MASTER BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

Mia looks up from her sewing as the TV reception steadies crystal clear to show -ON TV A TOY COMMERCIAL. Of a girl playing with her beloved doll. Mia smiles. But then wrinkles her nose. Like, what the hell is that smell? She leans out of her chair to get a view of the
HALLWAY
Sees a ROLLING CLOUD of BLACK SMOKE.
Oh God... Rises up out of her chair. Hurries down the hallway toward the
KITCHEN

PILLARS of FLAME erupt from the oven, licking the ceiling. The Ceiling Fan CRASHES at Mia's feet. She falls back. Lands hard. She grabs her stomach. Face twists in anguish as -Hot Flames closer. Black Smoke thicker. Mia begins to crawl toward the Front Door. Using every ounce of strength left in her. Is she going to make it? Don't think so... WHAM! Door busts OPEN. A NEIGHBOR stands at the threshold. Sees Mia on the floor -NEIGHBOR Help! She's still in here! Someone call an ambulance! Neighbor reaches down and begins to drag Mia outside. INT.
HOSPITAL - HALLWAY - LATER

John CRASHES through double doors. Tie loose around his neck, like a hangman's noose. Tears and sweat streak his face. When he nearly collides with a JANITOR -JOHN 307 -- Room 307 -Janitor motions to the end of the Hallway. John can't get there fast enough. INT.
ROOM 307 - SECONDS LATER

John rushes in. Sees Mia on the hospital bed. Hooked up to all sorts of machines that blip and bleep. She looks wrung out and hung out. Tries to pick her head up -- or smile -- or something -but she can only manage -MIA Hjhaee...
Hey you...
He leans over her. Brushes away the strands of hair plastered to her face.
I'm so sorry, baby. I'm so sorry I wasn't here...
Presses his lips against her.
Behind you...
John turns his head and sees -His NEWBORN BABY. Cradled by a Nurse. Pink. Healthy. Wrinkly.
Congratulations. It's a girl.
Nurse hands the baby to John. His eyes well up. So much happiness inside of him.
Hey there little...
Leah.
John smiles.
Hey there little Leah...
Mia watches her husband dote on their newborn daughter. Her smile dims when -MIA
Don't worry about it, Mia. I'm just glad you're both okay...
I don't know what happened -- it's like it's cursed -- I can't go back there again... Not with her...
John pulls his girls closer to him.
You don't have to...
EXT. PALMIRI APARTMENTS - DAY A 1940s ten story apartment building. Located on a tree lined street in beautiful downtown Pasadena.
Could be the cover of a visitor's brochure.
INT. PALMIRI - FORM APARTMENT - DAY

We're seven floors up. Two Bedroom. Exposed Brick. Hardwood floor. Crown moldings. It's spacious too. Or it would be if it wasn't for all those MOVING BOXES stacked like a failed game of Tetris. Somewhere among them, Leah CRIES. Mia moves to a blanket spread out on the floor. Leah lays there. Crying. Mia picks her up. Calms her down. As Leah quiets, the ceiling CREAKS above them. Mia looks up. Annoyed at the footsteps of the neighbors above. Apartment living takes some getting used to. John exits from the Bedroom. Like Mia and Leah, he's wearing his Sunday best...
All ready?
INT. SEVENTH FLOOR HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER

The Forms' wait for the ELEVATOR. It's one of those old ones with the SLIDING GRATING DOORS. Ding! Doors open, revealing -FULLER. Early 30s. Tall, scruffy. He could use a shower. Maybe two of them. Definitely went to Woodstock. Mia almost hesitant to get in until -FULLER Mr. Form. How are you, sir? John enters into the
ELEVATOR
Forces Mia to follow. It's about the size of a coffin. Making it a tight fit for two people. Let alone three and a baby.
Honey, this is... ...can't remember.
Joe Fuller. Hi, Ma'am. How are ya?
Nice to meet you. Doesn't sound all that convincing though. Something about that beard.. that hair... Reminds her of someone she'd rather forget.
He's our landlord. Fuck. Mia can't believe it either.
You're our landlord?
And building manager. So if you need anything you let me know...
His parents own this -FULLER -- my grandparents, actually. They own the building. Bought it back in -Jah-jerk.
Elevator stops. In-between floors.
What's happening?
Mia and John look at each other.
It does this from time to time. It's old so it pretty much has a mind of it's own...
Can't you fix it?
